label: PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning

node.label: PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning
node.definition: PDDL, STRIPS, and hierarchical task-network planning represent actions with preconditions and effects, then search for a valid plan from an initial state to a goal.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning is modeled as depending on Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Discrete mathematics & logic and feeding downstream nodes such as Formal verification of agents & plans.
examples: atlas downstream link to Formal verification of agents & plans
combined with Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Task decomposition & hierarchical reasoning, Discrete mathematics & logic.
